---
name: unit-tests-FIRST
description: Use when generating or reviewing unit tests. Defines the FIRST criteria that every generated unit test must satisfy. Apply to test planning, test writing, and test quality assessment.
---

## The FIRST Criteria

Every unit test generated by the Unit Test Generator **must** satisfy all five criteria below. Evaluate each criterion explicitly before writing the test. Document compliance in `test-report.md § FIRST Compliance`.

---

### F — Fast

**Definition**: Each test must complete in milliseconds. The full suite for a single bug fix should complete in under 2 seconds.

**Rules**:
- No real network calls (HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC). Mock or stub all external I/O.
- No real database calls. Use in-memory fakes or mocks.
- No `sleep()` / `setTimeout()` with real delays. If timing is needed, use fake timers (e.g. `jest.useFakeTimers()`).
- No file system reads/writes unless the function under test is explicitly a file-system function.

**Verification question**: *Will this test complete in <100 ms on any developer machine with no network?*

---

### I — Independent

**Definition**: Each test is fully self-contained. Tests can run in any order and produce the same result.

**Rules**:
- Each `it` / `test` block creates its own input data — no shared mutable state between tests.
- `beforeEach` / `afterEach` hooks reset all mocks and state before/after every individual test.
- No test depends on a side-effect produced by a previous test (e.g. a record inserted by test 1 that test 2 reads).
- Tests must not share module-level variables that are mutated during the test.

**Verification question**: *If I run only this one test in isolation, will it produce the same result as when it runs with the full suite?*

---

### R — Repeatable

**Definition**: Running the same test in any environment (developer machine, CI, container) at any time always produces identical results.

**Rules**:
- No dependency on system clock (`Date.now()`, `new Date()`) unless mocked.
- No dependency on random values (`Math.random()`) unless the seed is fixed.
- No dependency on environment variables that may differ across machines (use defaults or mocks).
- No dependency on execution order or external state that persists between runs.

**Verification question**: *If this test is run 100 times on 100 different machines, will it always return the same PASS or FAIL?*

---

### S — Self-validating

**Definition**: The test produces a clear, binary PASS or FAIL result via assertions. No human needs to interpret output.

**Rules**:
- Every test must contain at least one assertion (e.g. `expect(result).toBe(…)`, `assert.strictEqual(…)`).
- `console.log` inside a test body is forbidden — the assertion output must be the sole signal.
- Assertions must use specific matchers: prefer `.toBe()`, `.toEqual()`, `.toStrictEqual()` over broad `.toBeTruthy()` / `.toBeDefined()` unless the intent is explicitly truthiness.
- A test that always passes regardless of the code under test is invalid — each assertion must be capable of failing.

**Verification question**: *Does this test fail automatically (without human inspection) if the function returns a wrong value?*

---

### T — Timely

**Definition**: Tests are written for the code that was changed by the current fix — not as a retrofit for unrelated existing code.

**Rules**:
- Generate tests only for functions or code paths introduced or modified by the fix (as listed in `fix-summary.md § Changes Made`).
- Do not generate tests for functions that were untouched by the fix, even if those functions lack tests.
- Each test is written to catch the exact bug that was fixed (i.e. it would have **failed** before the fix and **passes** after).
- Include at least one test that directly exercises the fixed behaviour (regression guard).

**Verification question**: *Would this test have caught the bug before the fix was applied?*

---

## Quick Reference Card

| Letter | Criterion | Key question |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| **F** | Fast | Completes in <100 ms with no real I/O? |
| **I** | Independent | Passes when run alone, in any order? |
| **R** | Repeatable | Same result on every machine, every time? |
| **S** | Self-validating | Fails automatically if code is wrong? |
| **T** | Timely | Covers only the changed code; would have caught the bug? |

---

## Compliance Report Format

In `test-report.md § FIRST Compliance`, report each criterion as follows:

```
| Criterion     | Status | Notes                                              |
|---------------|--------|----------------------------------------------------|
| Fast          | ✅     | All tests mocked; suite runs in 0.3 s              |
| Independent   | ✅     | beforeEach resets all mocks; no shared state       |
| Repeatable    | ✅     | No Date.now() or Math.random(); no env vars used   |
| Self-validating | ✅   | All assertions use .toEqual() or .toBe()           |
| Timely        | ✅     | Tests cover getUserById only; would fail pre-fix   |
```

If any criterion is **❌**, explain why and what must be fixed before the test file is accepted.
